title: Question: Can Packages be dead-code-removed?
body:
---


Author Name: **Johan Bjork**
Original Redmine Message: 1799 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Any package that contains a non elaboration time function will remain in the Ast for all passes and finally will be written out as a .cpp file. 

Is this necessary? See for example t_package_abs.pl in the regression suite. In that example, the generated AstCFunc's does contain a reference to the AstScope inside the package (the only AstNode under the package module), but I'm not sure if it's ever used.
I'm asking because we have quite a lot of packages in our designs, resulting in ~200 almost empty classes in the final Verilated design. 

Can V3Dead be extended to support removing unnecessary package cells/modules?
